item.title: Mastodon says its flagship server was hit by a DDoS attack
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker, Sarah Perez
item.description: The DDoS attack against Mastodon's flagship server comes less than a week after Bluesky was targeted with junk web traffic.

item.title: FAA orders investigation into Blue Origin’s New Glenn mishap
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The New Glenn rocket will have to stay grounded while the company investigates what caused the apparent failure of its upper stage.

item.title: NSA spies are reportedly using Anthropic’s Mythos, despite Pentagon feud
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: NSA is said to be using Anthropic's restricted Mythos AI model.

item.title: WhatsApp is testing a premium subscription, but it is mainly cosmetic
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: WhatsApp is testing a subscription after Meta debuted a similar subscription for Instagram earlier this year

item.title: CEO and CFO suddenly depart AI nuclear power upstart Fermi
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: The startup, co-founded by former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry, has faced headwinds with its AI campus in Texas.

item.title: Deezer says 44% of songs uploaded to its platform daily are AI-generated
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Deezer says consumption of AI-generated music on the platform is still very low, between 1-3% of the total streams, and that 85% of these streams are detected as fraudulent and are demonetized.

item.title: App host Vercel says it was hacked and customer data stolen
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Vercel blamed its breach on an earlier hack at Context AI, which allowed hackers to hijack a Vercel employee's account to steal customer data.

item.title: Rivian’s factory hit by tornado ahead of R2 launch
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Nobody was hurt after an EF-1 tornado hit the part of the factory where the R2 SUV is supposed to be built.

item.title: OpenAI’s existential questions
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss OpenAI's latest acquisitions and whether they address "two big existential problems" for the company.

item.title: The 12-month window
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: A lot of AI startups exist partly because the foundation models haven't expanded into their category yet. As many jokingly acknowledge, that won't last forever.

item.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn put a customer satellite in the wrong orbit during its third launch
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The first major failure of Blue Origin's new heavy-launch system could create delays to its ambition to help NASA and the Trump administration return to the Moon.

item.title: Robots beat human records at Beijing half-marathon
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The winning time is a massive improvement over last year’s race, when the fastest robot finished in two hours and 40 minutes.

item.title: Palantir posts mini-manifesto denouncing inclusivity and ‘regressive’ cultures
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Palantir's ideological bent has come under more scrutiny as it's worked with ICE and positioned itself as a defender of "the West."

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Uber enters its assetmaxxing era
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

item.title: Cracks are starting to form on fusion energy’s funding boom
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Fusion startups and investors risk deepening fissures if some key disagreements aren't settled amicably.

item.title: Blue Origin successfully re-uses a New Glenn rocket for the first time ever
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: It's a major milestone for the company's new mega-rocket system, putting it in position to challenge SpaceX's dominance of the global launch market.

item.title: Tesla brings its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The company now offers robotaxi service in three cities, all of them in Texas, after launching in Austin last year and starting to offer rides without safety drivers in January 2026.

item.title: VC Ron Conway says he has a ‘rare form of cancer’
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Conway wrote that he “will be stepping back from some of my usual activities,” but he will “continue to support” founders backed by his firm SV Angel.

item.title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: In recent months, the company announced an agreement with Amazon Web Services to use Cerebras chips in Amazon data centers, as well as a deal with OpenAI reportedly worth more than $10 billion.

item.title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Despite recently being designated a supply-chain risk by the Pentagon, Anthropic is still talking to high-level members of the Trump administration.
